examples: The key to their approach was a statistical concept called heritability.
In broad terms, the heritability of a trait measures the extent to which differences among members of a population can be explained by differences in their genetics.
But perhaps we need to look again at the evidence for these high levels of heritability.
Twin studies are also unravelling the heritability of various aspects of human personality.
Autism and Asperger Syndrome (its (somewhat) less severe variant) are obviously genetic with incomplete penetrance and a heritability of 90%.
